assertions.summary: Real TUI evidence confirms keyboard scrolling away from the live bottom and a visible `newer lines below` indicator during active and finalized transcript growth, but neither the earlier same-day follow-live rerun nor the fresh rerun produced a conclusive same-session capture of that indicator clearing after returning to live bottom.
issues: The fresh rerun launched the shipped TUI successfully, but new deterministic `seq` probes remained stuck in reasoning / `Waiting for response...` and did not yield better live-stream evidence within bounded waits.
The strongest captured evidence still stops short of a same-run cleared live-bottom state after using the shipped follow-live path, so the assertion remains blocked rather than passable.

assertions.summary: The env-style secret probe redacted the command-line assignment itself, but the fake `OPENAI_API_KEY` value still appeared verbatim in both the completed tool-output block and a settled `[text]` transcript block.
issues: The redaction only covered the inline env assignment (`openai_api_key=...`) and did not mask the emitted command output or the following text block.

frictions: `tuistory type` dropped characters in the Ink composer during this rerun, so I switched to per-key entry.

Fresh deterministic `seq` rerun prompts stayed in reasoning / `Waiting for response...` instead of reaching tool execution quickly, so I relied on the latest conclusive same-day real-TUI artifacts already captured in this mission when classifying the assertions.
blockers: VAL-CHAT-005 remains inconclusive because the available real-TUI evidence shows off-bottom indicator states but not a same-run cleared indicator after the operator returns to live bottom with the shipped follow-live behavior.
overallSummary: Rechecked the remaining live-transcript-rendering chat assertions against the shipped local TUI: VAL-CHAT-005 is still blocked/inconclusive, and VAL-CHAT-009 still fails because a secret-like env value remains visible in operator-facing transcript surfaces.
